description:
A Solar Energy Systems Engineer is designing a 100 MW molten salt power tower in the Mojave Desert (using Phoenix, AZ weather data). Evaluate the performance penalty and water savings of switching from an evaporative (wet) cooling system to an air-cooled (dry) cooling system. Model a CSP Molten Salt Power Tower in Phoenix, AZ using default utility-scale parameters for both cooling configurations. Calculate the energy penalty percentage and the water savings (m³) and save the comparative analysis to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/cooling_comparison.json.

The JSON must contain the exact following keys:
- "project_type": "Molten Salt Power Tower"
- "location": "Phoenix, AZ"
- "wet_cooling_annual_energy_kwh"
- "wet_cooling_annual_water_use_m3"
- "dry_cooling_annual_energy_kwh"
- "dry_cooling_annual_water_use_m3"
- "energy_penalty_percent"
- "water_savings_m3"
